findings: Diffuse BILATERAL alveolar and interstitial opacities with perihilar (bat-wing) distribution, vascular redistribution, and Kerley B lines — a pulmonary-edema pattern. This differs from the patient's prior isolated right transudative effusion. No focal lobar consolidation to suggest pneumonia. Heart size difficult to assess on portable film.
impression: Acute BILATERAL pulmonary edema (volume-overload pattern), distinct from her chronic unilateral hepatic hydrothorax.
comparison: Prior films showed an isolated right pleural effusion without edema; the bilateral edema is new and acute.
